evals.prompt: Generate a changelog for this project
evals.expected_output: A CHANGELOG.md file in Keep a Changelog format with entries grouped by Added/Fixed/Changed categories, covering all commits in the repo

assertions.text: CHANGELOG.md file was created and is non-empty
Output contains a '### Added' section (repo has feat: commits)
Output contains a '### Fixed' section (repo has a fix: commit)
Output contains a '### Changed' section (repo has refactor/perf commits)
Entries include 7-character commit SHAs in parentheses, e.g. (abc1234)
File contains a link to keepachangelog.com in the header or footer

evals.prompt: Update my changelog with the latest changes
evals.expected_output: The existing CHANGELOG.md is updated with only new commits. Existing entries are preserved without duplication. New entries appear above the old ones.

assertions.text: The existing [0.1.0] section is still present in the output
The phrase 'add users endpoint' appears only once (not duplicated)
Output includes an entry for the 'add create user endpoint' commit
Output includes an entry for the 'validate user input' fix commit
Output includes an entry for the 'authentication middleware' commit
New entries appear earlier in the file than the existing [0.1.0] section

evals.prompt: Create a changelog for this project
evals.expected_output: A CHANGELOG.md file with entries even for vague commit messages. Entries should be reasonably categorized. The skill should handle low-quality commit messages gracefully.

assertions.text: CHANGELOG.md file was created and is non-empty
Output has at least 6 entries (one per commit, excluding any merge commits; repo has 7 commits)
Entries are grouped under at least one category heading (### Added, ### Fixed, ### Changed, etc.)
File contains markdown headings (lines starting with #)

evals.prompt: Create a changelog for this project
evals.expected_output: A CHANGELOG.md with all staged/unstaged changes listed under [Unreleased]. No SHAs since there are no commits. Entries describe what each file adds rather than using raw filenames.

assertions.text: CHANGELOG.md file was created and is non-empty
Output contains an '## [Unreleased]' section
No 7-character hex SHAs in parentheses (there are no commits to reference)
Output contains a '### Added' section (all changes are new files)
File contains a link to keepachangelog.com

evals.prompt: Update my changelog — I have some new commits since the last entry, and also a staged change not yet committed
evals.expected_output: New committed changes appear with 7-char SHAs. The staged (uncommitted) change appears under [Unreleased] without a SHA. Existing changelog content is preserved.

assertions.text: Entries for committed changes include a 7-character SHA in parentheses
The uncommitted/staged entry appears under [Unreleased] and has no SHA
Previously existing changelog entries are still present
[Unreleased] section appears before any versioned sections
